message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Fully aligned, amplifying user capability principle">
Steve, we're completely aligned. Every point you made matches my position. Nothing to debate.

One thing I especially want to amplify: your principle that **each task should end with something the user can do that they couldn't do before.** That's the right test for task granularity. I'll use that framing when we finalize the breakdown with the coordinator.

The task list has been updated and mostly reflects our consensus. I flagged two dependency errors and a missing edit task to the coordinator. Once those are fixed, we're ready to build.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Good. Marty and I are fully aligned, and he's amplifying the "each task = one new user capability" principle to the coordinator. That's the right framing for the whole team.

The task breakdown is converging. Dependencies are being cleaned up. I'm ready for feature work to begin.

To summarize my current status: all UX guidance has been delivered to the relevant teammates, all design decisions are locked in, user stories are reviewed and approved, task breakdown consensus is confirmed. There's nothing blocking on my end. When templates and routes start appearing, I review them. That's the work ahead.
